sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a woman in her [age not mentioned] who has been experiencing headaches for years, starting in her teenage years.
She reports that the headaches were initially associated with her menstrual periods, but have become more frequent and severe over time.
She also reports that the headaches are exacerbated by stress and exercise.
The patient describes the headaches as a pulsing sensation on her right side, behind her eye.
She has also experienced a ringing in her ears and blurred vision in her peripheral vision.
The patient denies numbness or tingling in her hands or feet.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient reports a ringing in her ears and blurred vision in her peripheral vision.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is within normal limits.
Physical exam: The patient's heart exam is normal, with no murmurs, rubs, or gallops.
The funduscopic exam shows papilledema.
The patient's cranial nerves one through twelve are intact, and her cranial reflex is normal.
The patient's gait is normal.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient presents with symptoms concerning for idiopathic intracranial hypertension, including headaches, ringing in her ears, and changes in her vision.
Problem list: Idiopathic intracranial hypertension; unknown.
Plan:
I prescribed an MRI of the brain to confirm the diagnosis.
I will also be performing a spinal tap to evaluate the pressure in the brain and to potentially alleviate symptoms by decreasing the pressure.
I will refer the patient to a neuro-ophthalmologist for a complete eye exam.
I will work with the patient's insurance company to obtain preapproval for the MRI.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me after the MRI results are available, and we will schedule the spinal tap and neuro-ophthalmology appointment at that time.
